name: Kaya Holdings, Inc.
business: Kaya Holdings, Inc is a holding company focusing on wellness and mental health through operations in psychedelic treatment clinics, medical and recreational cannabis, and CBD products.
sellsTo: individuals seeking psilocybin treatments for mental health disorders
medical cannabis patients and recreational users in legal markets
veterans suffering from PTSD, addicts, individuals with eating disorders

exposures.sentence: KAYS is operating The Sacred Mushroom as part of its Fifth Dimension Therapeutics, Inc. subsidiary ( FDT ), which also intends to work cooperatively with select pharmaceutical companies to maximize the curative and therapeutic potential of psilocybin.

During the last 10 years of cannabis operations the Company has produced, distributed, and/or sold a full range of premium cannabis products including flower, oils, vape cartridges and cannabis infused confections, baked goods and beverages through a fully integrated group of subsidiaries and companies supporting highly distinctive brands.

catalysts.event: Received license for psilocybin treatment facility from OHA
catalysts.sentence: We received our license for our psilocybin treatment facility from the OHA in April 2024 and began providing treatments in September 2024.

catalysts.event: Suspended payroll due to capital constraints
catalysts.sentence: In April 2025, we suspended payroll to our employees due to capital constraints and we are evaluating the operational structure of the facility with a view to restructuring operations in order to generate greater revenues.

tamClaims.claim: Global cannabis market expected to reach $74 billion by 2029 with CAGR of 3.01%.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Statista, cannabis is expected to reach a legal market of 74 billion USD by 2029, for a CAGR of 3.01%.

tamClaims.claim: By 2024, forecasted global cannabis market of $103.9 billion, with Europe $39.1 billion and North America $37.9 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: By 2024, with a forecasted global market of $103.9 billion, Europe is expected to outperform North America $39.1 billion to $37.9 billion.
